page: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. System memory is configured at 8.0 GB, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with bzip3 single-threaded decompression reaching top-tier levels at 14.78 GB/sec and gzip single-threaded compression performing strongly at 34.6 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is weak at 4513.8, and memory bandwidth write operations for large blocks are also weak at 23.19 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores sit in the average tier, as do Redis SET operations at 8.2 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 8.6 GB/sec.

The D4ls_v7 exhibits a clear tradeoff between high single-core execution speed and constrained multi-threaded scaling and memory bandwidth. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low-memory profile reduces resource density costs for workloads that do not require large memory footprints but benefit from dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores. This server is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, lightweight database operations, and small-scale model inference, while it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or heavy multi-threaded parallel computing.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. This low-memory configuration lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Single-core performance is a key strength, with top-tier single-threaded bzip3 decompression and strong gzip compression. Conversely, multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-focused tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. It is suited for lightweight database operations, static web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is a low-memory, x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded performance.
tagline: A dedicated compute-focused virtual machine featuring high-frequency single-core performance and a low-memory profile for cost-efficient processing.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Top-tier single-threaded decompression and strong compression speeds
No local storage included, but Premium Storage capable
Suited for static web serving, lightweight databases, and single-threaded tasks
